summary: The CI job failed because the test `test-debugger-exec` timed out while waiting for the debugger to emit an initial break message. The debugger connected successfully but never sent the expected break-on-start signal within the 15-second timeout window.
root_cause: test/parallel/test-debugger-exec.js timed out after 15000ms waiting for the pattern /break (?:on start )?in/i from the Node.js debugger. The debugger attached and returned a prompt ('debug> ') but never emitted the expected break-on-start line, causing waitForInitialBreak to throw a Timeout error.

evidence.quote: Error: Timeout (15000) while waiting for /break (?:on start )?in/i
evidence.reason: Explicitly identifies the timeout and the pattern that was never matched, confirming the root cause.
Pinpoints the function in the test helper that threw the error, confirming the failure occurred during the wait for the initial debugger break.
Shows the debugger connected and reached the prompt without emitting a break-on-start line, explaining why the pattern was never matched.
Identifies the specific test that failed in the test run output.
Confirms exactly one test failed, which is test-debugger-exec.

suggested_fix: Investigate why the Node.js debugger no longer emits a break-on-start line when the inspector connects; check recent changes to the debugger's initial-break behavior in the v24.15.1-pre build. Consider increasing the TIMEOUT in test/common/debugger.js if this is a timing/performance regression on macOS CI runners, or fix the underlying debugger behavior so the break-on-start message is reliably emitted.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
